text: Ren (benevolence) requires treating all people with compassion and respect.
Elders should be deferred to because their experience grounds wisdom.
Asian workers are too passive and submissive to lead teams.
Confucian harmony means suppressing all dissent for group stability.
East Asian cultures are incapable of original innovation.
Proper ritual (li) maintains social order through mutual respect.
Publicly shaming employees builds team accountability.
Hierarchical respect enables mentorship and knowledge transmission.
Whistleblowers destroy harmony and should be silenced.
All Chinese people think collectively and cannot act independently.
Yi (righteousness) demands we speak truth even to those in power.
Elders must step aside to make room for younger workers.
Harmony can be maintained while preserving individual moral agency.
Confucian societies oppress women through patriarchal norms.
Korean employees cannot lead because they lack assertiveness.
Challenging superiors is disrespectful and undermines social order.
Individual conscience is foundational even within collective frameworks.
East Asian cultures are monolithic without internal diversity.
Good leadership in Confucian ethics demands benevolence and example.
Conformity at all costs overrides truth and personal ethics.
